mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer demand, which forces equity markets to price in prolonged earnings declines, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending recovery, which directly lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative 20-day return in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply chain contraction and falling factory utilization, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with > 55% accuracy during crisis periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the GDP growth signal remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth direction across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low inflation readings and a declining federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, which reduces the cost of capital and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative 20-day return in > 50% of cases, or the real rate differential fails to explain variance in forward returns (adjusted R-squared < 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline exceeding 400,000 jobs indicates acute labor market stress and imminent wage deflation, which forces equity markets to downgrade earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in a walk-forward regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600,000 units during a credit crunch signal a prolonged real estate downturn, which drags down construction and consumer durables spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with > 55% accuracy during credit-constrained periods, or the predictive relationship reverses when housing inventory data is introduced.
